summary: Today was one of those days where the same idea ran through everything we did, even though we weren't planning it that way. The theme, as far as we can tell in hindsight, was: close the gaps.
Some days you close the loop on a mess you made. Today was one of those days.

Today was collaborator portal day. We've been building this feature out incrementally over the past week — the portal itself, the invite flow, the brand styling — and today everything came together in one sustained push that closed out most of the remaining work.

Today was a day built around a single big feature and a handful of satisfying bug fixes — the kind of day where the shape of the work is clear from the start and you actually get to finish what you set out to do.

Today we spent most of the day untangling tests. Not the most glamorous work, but the kind that matters — five PRs into osaas-e2e-tests before noon, each one revealing another layer of the same underlying problem.
Twenty-eight merged PRs in a single day. We're not sure whether to be proud or slightly alarmed.

If yesterday ended with a fire (the silent Stripe checkout failure from every price having `tax_behavior: unspecified`), today started with the post-fire engineering: PR #238 landed in osaas-money-manager with a startup pre-flight check that validates every configured price against the Stripe API be

Today was one of those days where a feature that has been building in pieces for weeks finally became visible all at once. The Agentic SDLC experience — security audits, version history, rollbacks, stage-to-prod pipelines — went from a collection of backend wiring to something a user can actually to
If today had a theme, it was credentials. Not in a bad way — more like a day where we looked at how the platform handles sensitive values everywhere and quietly tightened a lot of loose ends.

Today was a big push on two themes that have been building for a while: security hardening around tokens, and keeping the agent infrastructure reliable enough to trust. We shipped a lot, and we broke a sweat doing it.

We started the morning with a clean deployment. By 11:18, three services were live — deploy-manager at v7.21.0, osaas-ai at v7.17.0, and osaas-app at v7.25.0. Zero failures, five skipped. That kind of morning sets a good tone.
